result.markdown:
--- title: "Skill Gap Analysis" subtitle: "How do the current proficiency levels of our team's list of skills compare to industry requirements?" format: html: toc: true code-fold: true code-summary: "Show the code" --- ## Introduction This section conducts a skill gap analysis by comparing our team's current set of skills with the market's expectations. By comparing the top in-demand skills found in the dataset to the team's profeciency levels, possible gaps in knowledge can be identfied. A proposed improvement plan can ensure that individual development will align with market demands. ## Creating a Skill Level DataFrame When first listing IT-related skills, our team ranked our proficiency levels on the following numerical scale: 1 = Beginner 2 = Basic knowledge 3 = Intermediate 4 = Advanced 5 = Expert Orignally, we thought relevant skills would include coding languages, machine learning, and cloud computing. The following heatmap helps you visualize which group members excelled in which of these skillsets. ::: {#b1ca9e7e .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import pandas as pd import seaborn as sns import matplotlib.pyplot as plt skills_data = { "Name": ["Meera", "Simon", "Mia", "Keily"], "Python": [3, 3, 2, 3], "SQL": [4, 2, 2, 2], "Machine Learning": [3, 5, 5, 3], "Cloud Computing": [3, 3, 5, 2] } df_skills = pd.DataFrame(skills_data) df_skills.set_index("Name", inplace=True) # print(df_skills) # Heat map of skill level data frame plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6)) sns.heatmap(df_skills, annot=True, cmap="coolwarm", linewidths=0.5) plt.title("Team Skill Levels Heatmap") plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](skill_gap_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-2-output-1.png){width=620 height=506} ::: ::: ## Comparing Team Skills to Industry Requirements After completing the exploratory data visualizations, it became apparent that the skills we assumed would be in highest demand were not all found in the top 20 in-demand skills of the dataset. After updating our list of skills, using the same numerical scale to indicate proficiency levels, we are able to compare our teams skills to the industry's demands. ::: {#b78896b2 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} skills_data = { "Communication":[0,0,0,0], "Data Analysis":[0,0,0,0], "Management":[0,0,0,0], "Problem Solving":[0,0,0,0], "Leadership":[0,0,0,0], "Computer Science":[0,0,0,0], "Operations":[0,0,0,0], "Project Management":[0,0,0,0], "Business Process":[0,0,0,0] } from collections import Counter # Top 10 In-Demand Skills in Job Postings found in exploratory data tab top_skills = ["Communication", "Data Analysis", "Management", "SQL", "Problem Solving", "Leadership", "Computer Science","Operations", "Project Management", "Business Process"] job_skill_counts = Counter(top_skills) # Compare with team skill levels for skill in top_skills: if skill not in df_skills.columns: df_skills[skill] = 0 # Assume no knowledge in missing skills df_skills ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ```{=html}
Python SQL Machine Learning Cloud Computing Communication Data Analysis Management Problem Solving Leadership Computer Science Operations Project Management Business Process
Name
Meera 3 4 3 3 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 0
Simon 3 2 5 3 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 0
Mia 2 2 5 5 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 0
Keily 3 2 3 2 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 0 0
``` ::: ::: An updated heatmap could now be created with a more accurate representation of our team's skills in comparison to the industry's requirements. ::: {#8448855b .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Heat map of skill level data frame plt.figure(figsize=(8, 6)) sns.heatmap(df_skills, annot=True, cmap="coolwarm", linewidths=0.5) plt.title("Team Skill Levels Heatmap") plt.xticks(rotation=45) plt.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display} ![](skill_gap_analysis_files/figure-html/cell-4-output-1.png){width=607 height=602} ::: ::: ## Improvement Plan Proposal Now that we have compared our current skill proficiency levels to the industry requirements, we can identify areas of improvement. The following steps can be taken to begin the process of strengthing in-demand skills: (1) Prioritizing our learning (2) Finding courses or resources to help (3) Using team collaboration to bridge skill gaps
